description: Fix genuine bugs found during browser extension audit: failed plugins hidden during search, misleading confirmation scope option when domain is null, and duplicate utility function.

userStories.title: Pass failedPlugins through SearchResults to PluginList
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see failed plugins in search results so that I can find and troubleshoot failing plugins even when a search query is active.
acceptanceCriteria: SearchResults passes the received failedPlugins prop to PluginList instead of hardcoded []
When a search query is active and a plugin has a load error, the failed plugin card is visible in the 'Installed' section of search results
When there is no search query, failed plugins still appear in the main view as before
userStories.notes:
In platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/SearchResults.tsx, the `failedPlugins` prop is declared in `SearchResultsProps` (line 12), destructured from props (line 43 area — it's actually not destructured, it's omitted from the destructuring), and then `PluginList` at line 94 receives `failedPlugins={[]}` instead of the actual prop value.

The fix: destructure `failedPlugins` from props and pass it to `PluginList` at line 94 instead of `[]`. The `failedPlugins` should also be filtered by the search query — only show failed plugins whose specifier or error message matches the filter. Use a simple substring match on the specifier (which is the plugin's npm package name or local path). The `FailedPluginState` type has `specifier` and `error` fields.

Also update `hasInstalledResults` (line 72) to include failed plugin matches so the 'Installed' section header shows when only failed plugins match the search.

Do NOT change the PluginList component — it already accepts and renders failedPlugins correctly.

userStories.title: Hide 'For this tool on this domain' scope when domain is null
userStories.description: As a user, I want the confirmation dialog to only show domain-scoped options when a domain is available, so that I don't create invalid allow-always rules.
acceptanceCriteria: When a confirmation request has domain=null, the 'For this tool on this domain' menu item is not rendered
When a confirmation request has a non-null domain string, 'For this tool on this domain' is rendered as before
The 'For this tool everywhere' option (tool_all scope) is always available regardless of domain
The 'For all tools on {domain}' option (domain_all scope) is only shown when domain is non-null (already correct)
userStories.notes:
In platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ConfirmationDialog.tsx, the `AllowAlwaysButton` component (lines 56-70) always shows 'For this tool on this domain' at line 65, regardless of whether `domain` is null. Only the 'For all tools on {domain}' option at line 67 is conditionally rendered.

The fix: wrap the 'For this tool on this domain' Menu.Item (line 65) with a `{domain && ...}` conditional, the same pattern used on line 67.

When domain is null and only 'For this tool everywhere' remains, the AllowAlwaysButton still needs to show at least one option. If domain is null, only the `tool_all` option should be visible. The dropdown with a single option is still useful — it communicates the permanent nature of the action.

Do NOT change the scope types or the server-side confirmation handling.

userStories.title: Remove duplicate deriveDisplayName in NpmPluginCard
userStories.description: As a developer, I want duplicated utility functions to be consolidated so that name extraction logic stays consistent across the codebase.
acceptanceCriteria: NpmPluginCard imports and uses extractShortName from bridge.ts instead of its local deriveDisplayName
The local deriveDisplayName function is deleted from NpmPluginCard.tsx
NpmPluginCard renders the same display name as before (extractShortName has identical logic)
userStories.notes:
In platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/NpmPluginCard.tsx, the `deriveDisplayName` function (lines 20-21) implements the exact same logic as `extractShortName` in platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.ts (line 73):

Both do: `(name.split('/').pop() ?? name).replace(/^opentabs-plugin-/, '')`

The fix:
1. Add `extractShortName` to the existing import from '../bridge' (line 6 already imports PluginSearchResult)
2. Replace `deriveDisplayName(plugin.name)` with `extractShortName(plugin.name)` at line 24
3. Delete the `deriveDisplayName` function and its JSDoc comment (lines 15-21)

This is the final story in the PRD and touches UI code, so e2eCheckpoint is true.
